title: FetchNodeOutput
description: Output for core.openwop.http.fetch. Carries the response and per-attempt telemetry so downstream nodes can branch on status / inspect bodies / observe retry behaviour.

status.description: Final HTTP status code (after retries, if any). The sentinel value `0` indicates network exhaustion — no HTTP response was ever received across all retry attempts. When `status === 0`, the `networkError` field carries the last error's message. Workflow authors checking for success should test `status >= 200 && status < 300`.

headers.description: Final response headers. Header names are lowercased per the Fetch standard. The host MAY strip headers it considers sensitive before persisting; consumers MUST NOT rely on every transport header being present.

body.description: Response body. Parsed as JSON when `Content-Type` includes `application/json` and the body parses successfully; otherwise the raw UTF-8 string. Empty responses produce `null`.

durationMs.description: Total wall-clock duration from first attempt to terminal response, including backoff delays.

attempts.description: Number of attempts made. `1` means the first attempt succeeded; higher values mean retries fired.

idempotencyKey.description: The `Idempotency-Key` header value sent on this request (when idempotency mode is `auto` and method is non-idempotent). Surfaced for replay-determinism debugging.

networkError.description: Set ONLY when every attempt failed with a network error (no HTTP response was ever received) — in that case `status` is `0`. Carries the last error's message for diagnostic surfacing. Absent on any non-zero `status`.
